notion_description: Vertical picture graph appears on left. Scale of 2. Each symbol = 2.
Vertical picture graph appears on right. Scale of 5. Each symbol = 5. Key highlighted.
Key on scale of 5 graph highlights.
Key on scale of 5 graph highlights.
Key on scale of 5 graph highlights.
Key highlights showing Each ⭐ = 5. One symbol pulses to demonstrate the connection.
Data table appears. Favorite Playground Activities. Swings: 20, Slides: 15, Monkey Bars: 25, Sandbox: 10. Vertical orientation.
20 items appear for Swings category. Key visible: Each ⭐ = 5.
20 items cluster into 4 groups of 5.
4 groups transform into 4 symbols, stacking upward on picture graph for Swings column.
Vertical picture graph appears. Swings column has 4 symbols. Other columns empty. Key visible: Each symbol = 5.
Data table appears alongside picture graph showing same playground data.
Swings column highlights.
Key highlights on picture graph.
All 4 symbols in Swings column highlight.
System highlights each symbol in sequence while counting: 5, 10, 15, 20.

Vertical picture graph appears in building mode. Favorite Playground Activities. Scale 5 (Each symbol = 5). Swings column has 4 symbols already placed. Slides, Monkey Bars, Sandbox columns empty. Key visible.

Vertical picture graph appears in building mode. Favorite Playground Equipment data. Swings has 4 symbols (20 votes), Slides has 3 symbols (15 votes). Monkey Bars and Sandbox rows are empty. Key visible: each symbol = 5 votes.
Data table appears alongside graph showing Favorite Playground Equipment values: Swings 20, Slides 15, Monkey Bars 25, Sandbox 10.

Vertical picture graph appears. Favorite Playground Equipment data. Swings column has 4 symbols, Slides column has 3 symbols, Monkey Bars column has 5 symbols, Sandbox column empty. Key: each symbol = 5 votes.
Vertical picture graph appears. Favorite Playground Activities data. Swings=4 symbols, Slides=3 symbols, Monkey Bars=5 symbols, Sandbox=2 symbols. Key visible: each symbol = 5 votes.
Monkey Bars column highlights as system demonstrates.
Previous highlight clears.
Sandbox column highlights as system demonstrates.

Vertical picture graph appears on left side. Favorite Playground Activities data. Swings 4 symbols, Slides 3 symbols, Monkey Bars 5 symbols, Sandbox 2 symbols. Scale 5: each symbol = 5 votes. Key visible.
Vertical picture graph appears. Favorite Playground Activities data. Swings=4 symbols, Slides=3, Monkey Bars=5, Sandbox=2. Key visible: Each ⭐ = 5.
Data table appears showing Playground Activities values: Swings=20, Slides=15, Monkey Bars=25, Sandbox=10.
Picture graph transforms into vertical bar graph. Categories: Swings=20, Slides=15, Monkey Bars=25, Sandbox=10. Axis labeled 0–30 in 5s. All 4 bars pre-filled.
Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to where the scale is shown.
Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to the scale.
Animation traces up the axis, highlighting each number: 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30.
Vertical picture graph titled Favorite Playground Activities. Categories: Swings (4 symbols), Slides (3 symbols), Monkey Bars (5 symbols), Sandbox (2 symbols). Scale 5: each symbol = 5. Key visible.
Slides category highlights on picture graph.
Slides category highlights on bar graph.
Slides bar highlights. Axis markings pulse at 5, 10, 15.
Slides bar highlights. Guideline draws from bar top to axis at 15.
Slides bar pulses to draw attention. Other bars dim.
Guideline draws from top of Slides bar across to axis, landing at 15.
Data table appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Pizza=20, Tacos=30, Salad=15, Pasta=25, Sandwiches=15.
Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Axis labeled 0-35 in 5s. Tacos bar pre-filled at 30, Sandwiches bar pre-filled at 15. Pizza, Salad, Pasta bars empty.
Numbers 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 pulse on the axis to show the pattern.
Numbers 15, 20, 25 pulse to show the spacing between marks.
All axis numbers from 0 to 20 pulse to show the consistent interval.
Axis numbers highlight in sequence: 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, showing the counting by 5s pattern.
Vertical bar graph appears in building mode. Favorite Lunch Foods. Tacos bar pre-filled at 30, Sandwiches at 15. Pizza, Salad, Pasta bars empty. Scale: 5. Axis shows 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30.
Pizza bar confirms at height 20.
The number 20 highlights on the axis.
Pizza bar extends upward to height 20, demonstrating correct placement.
Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Pizza bar at 20, Tacos at 30, Sandwiches at 15. Salad and Pasta bars empty, ready to build.
Salad and Pasta bars confirm at correct heights.
Axis numbers highlight to show the scale increments.
System demonstrates: Salad bar rises to 15 on the axis, then Pasta bar rises to 25.
Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Pizza=20, Tacos=30, Salad=15, Pasta=25, Sandwiches=15.
Vertical picture graph appears alongside bar graph. Same Favorite Lunch Foods data. Each symbol = 5. Pizza=4 symbols, Tacos=6 symbols, Salad=3 symbols, Pasta=5 symbols, Sandwiches=3 symbols.
Data table appears showing Favorite Lunch Foods values.
All highlights clear.
All highlights clear.
System counts Pasta symbols by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25. Each symbol lights up as counted.
Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Tacos=30, Pizza=25, Salad=15, Pasta=20, Sandwiches=15. Tacos and Salad bars highlighted.
Tacos and Salad bars pulse to draw attention.
Tacos and Salad bars pulse.
Tacos and Salad bars pulse.
Visual bracket appears connecting Tacos bar at 30 and Salad bar at 15, showing the gap of 15.
Vertical data table appears. Books Read This Month. Liam=35, Emma=20, Noah=25, Olivia=40.
Horizontal picture graph appears. Books Read This Month. 0 categories filled. Key visible: Each ⭐ = 5 books.
7 symbols confirm in Liam row.
Liam's value 35 and the key highlights in data table.
7 symbols appear one by one in Liam row, skip-counting by 5s as each appears: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35.
Horizontal picture graph appears. Books Read This Month data. Liam row filled with 7 symbols. Emma, Noah, Olivia rows empty. Key: each symbol = 5 books.
Data table appears alongside graph showing Books Read This Month values: Liam 35, Emma 20, Noah 25, Olivia 40.
Data table rows highlight for Emma, Noah, and Olivia. Key highlights as well.
Emma row fills with 4 symbols, Noah row with 5 symbols, Olivia row with 8 symbols.

Horizontal picture graph appears. Books Read This Month data. Liam=7 symbols (35 books), Emma=4 symbols (20 books), Noah=5 symbols (25 books), Olivia=8 symbols (40 books). Key visible: each symbol = 5 books.

System highlights Emma's row showing 4 symbols, counts by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20. Then highlights Olivia's row showing 8 symbols, counts by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. Visual shows subtraction: 40 minus 20 equals 20.

Horizontal picture graph appears titled Books Read This Month. Scale 5: each symbol = 5 books. Four rows show: Liam 7 symbols (35 books), Emma 4 symbols (20 books), Noah 5 symbols (25 books), Olivia 8 symbols (40 books). Key visible. Scale toggle control displays three options: Each symbol = 1, Each symbol = 2, Each symbol = 5. Defaults to scale of 5.
Graph animates: symbols multiply. Olivia goes from 8 symbols to 40, Liam from 7 to 35, Noah from 5 to 25, Emma from 4 to 20. Key updates to Each symbol = 1.
Graph animates to scale of 1. Symbols multiply: Olivia from 8 to 40, Liam from 7 to 35, Noah from 5 to 25, Emma from 4 to 20. Key updates to Each symbol = 1.

Graph animates: symbols reduce. Olivia goes from 40 symbols to 20, Liam from 35 to 17.5 (half-symbol appears), Noah from 25 to 12.5 (half-symbol appears), Emma from 20 to 10. Key updates to Each symbol = 2.
Graph animates to scale of 2. Symbols reduce: Olivia from 40 to 20, Liam from 35 to 17.5 with half-symbol, Noah from 25 to 12.5 with half-symbol, Emma from 20 to 10. Key updates to Each symbol = 2.
Graph animates back to original state. Olivia goes from 20 symbols to 8, Liam from 17.5 to 7, Noah from 12.5 to 5, Emma from 10 to 4. Key updates to Each symbol = 5.
Graph animates to scale of 5. Symbols reduce: Olivia from 20 to 8, Liam from 17.5 to 7, Noah from 12.5 to 5, Emma from 10 to 4. Key updates to Each symbol = 5.
Horizontal bar graph appears. Bars extend sideways from left. Axis along bottom labeled 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45. All 4 bars empty.
Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to the scale pattern.
Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to the sequence.
Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to the direction.
Axis numbers highlight in sequence: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40.
Data table appears. This Week's Screen Time (minutes). Saturday=35, Sunday=30, Monday=40, Tuesday=25.
Empty horizontal bar graph template appears. Weekend Screen Time. Scale marked 0, 5, 10... up to 45.
Axis numbers highlight to show the skip-counting pattern.
System demonstrates setting all four bars. Saturday bar extends to 35, Sunday bar extends to 30, Monday bar extends to 40, Tuesday bar extends to 25.
Horizontal bar graph appears. This Week's Screen Time data. Saturday=35, Sunday=30, Monday=40, Tuesday=25.
Saturday and Sunday bars highlight.
Saturday and Sunday bars pulse to draw attention to both.
Saturday and Sunday bars highlight separately, one after the other.
System traces Saturday bar from 0 to 35, then Sunday bar from 0 to 30, showing the values on axis.
Horizontal picture graph titled 'Books Read This Month' appears. Four categories: Liam 7 symbols, Emma 4 symbols, Noah 5 symbols, Olivia 8 symbols. Key visible: each symbol = 5 books.
Categories are highlighted in both the graphs to draw student’s attention to the category count on both.
